title: गीता सार
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गीता सार ॥

धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः।
मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय॥ १॥

यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥ २॥

परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे॥ ३॥

कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥ ४॥

योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते॥ ५॥

नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः॥ ६॥

वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही॥ ७॥

उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्।
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥ ८॥

मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।
मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः॥ ९॥

सर्वधर्मान् परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥ १०॥

यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः।
तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री गीता सार सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gita Sara ||

Dharma-kshetre Kuru-kshetre samaveta yuyutsavah |
Mamakah Pandavash-chaiva kim-akurvata Sanjaya || 1 ||

Yada yada hi Dharmasya glanir-bhavati Bharata |
Abhyutthanam-adharmasya tada-atmanam srijamy-aham || 2 ||

Paritranaya sadhunam vinashaya cha dushkritam |
Dharma-samsthapana-arthaya sambhavami yuge yuge || 3 ||

Karmanye-vadhikaraste ma phaleshu kadachana |
Ma karma-phala-hetur-bhur-ma te sango'stv-akarmani || 4 ||

Yoga-sthah kuru karmani sangam tyaktva Dhananjaya |
Siddhy-asiddhyoh samo bhutva samatvam yoga uchyate || 5 ||

Nainam chhindanti shastrani nainam dahati pavakah |
Na chainam kledayanty-apo na shoshayati marutah || 6 ||

Vasamsi jirnani yatha vihaya navani grihnati naro'parani |
Tatha sharirani vihaya jirnany-anyani samyati navani dehi || 7 ||

Uddhared-atman-atmanam na-atmanam-avasadayet |
Atma-eva hy-atmano bandhur-atma-eva ripur-atmanah || 8 ||

Man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru |
Mam-evaishyasi yuktva-evam-atmanam mat-parayanah || 9 ||

Sarva-dharman parityajya mam-ekam sharanam vraja |
Aham tva sarva-papebhyo mokshayishyami ma shuchah || 10 ||

Yatra Yogeshvarah Krishno yatra Partho dhanur-dharah |
Tatra shrir-vijayo bhutir-dhruva nitir-matir-mama || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Gita Sara Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
धर्मक्षेत्र कुरुक्षेत्र में एकत्र हुए युद्ध की इच्छा वाले मेरे और पाण्डुपुत्रों ने क्या किया, हे संजय?॥ १॥

हे भारत! जब-जब धर्म की हानि और अधर्म की वृद्धि होती है, तब-तब मैं स्वयं को प्रकट करता हूँ॥ २॥

साधुओं की रक्षा, दुष्टों के विनाश और धर्म की स्थापना के लिए मैं युगे-युगे प्रकट होता हूँ॥ ३॥

तुम्हारा कर्म करने में ही अधिकार है, फल में कभी नहीं। कर्मफल का हेतु मत बनो, अकर्म में भी आसक्ति मत रखो॥ ४॥

हे धनञ्जय! योग में स्थित होकर, संग त्यागकर कर्म करो। सिद्धि-असिद्धि में सम होना ही योग कहलाता है॥ ५॥

इस आत्मा को शस्त्र काट नहीं सकते, अग्नि जला नहीं सकता, जल गीला नहीं कर सकता, वायु सुखा नहीं सकती॥ ६॥

जैसे मनुष्य पुराने वस्त्र त्यागकर नये धारण करता है, वैसे ही देही पुराने शरीर त्यागकर नये शरीर धारण करता है॥ ७॥

अपने आत्मा से अपना उद्धार करो, आत्मा को गिरने मत दो। आत्मा ही आत्मा का बन्धु है और आत्मा ही शत्रु है॥ ८॥

मुझमें मन लगाओ, मेरे भक्त बनो, मेरा यजन करो, मुझे नमस्कार करो। इस प्रकार मत्परायण होकर तुम मुझे ही प्राप्त होगे॥ ९॥

सब धर्मों को त्यागकर मेरी एकमात्र शरण में आओ। मैं तुम्हें सब पापों से मुक्त करूँगा, शोक मत करो॥ १०॥

जहाँ योगेश्वर कृष्ण हैं और जहाँ धनुर्धर अर्जुन हैं, वहाँ श्री, विजय, विभूति और ध्रुव नीति है — यह मेरा मत है॥ ११॥
benefits: Contains the supreme essence of all Bhagavad Gita teachings
Invokes clarity of purpose and righteous action (karma yoga)
Traditionally recited for removal of confusion about duty and spiritual path
Invokes the blessing of knowledge of the immortal self (atman)
Inspires fearlessness and equanimity in all situations
Daily recitation brings spiritual wisdom and inner peace
description: Gita Sara (गीता सार) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Krishna. Drawn from Bhagavad Gita - Mahabharata, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant clarity of purpose and righteous action (Karma Yoga) as well as inspire fearlessness and equanimity in all situations. Additionally, it is known to bestow knowledge of the immortal Self (Atman). For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar), Thursday (Guruvar), and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Gita Jayanti and Janmashtami. At 11 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 7 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Gita Sara in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Gita Sara - the essence of Bhagavad Gita in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Key verses of Lord Krishna's teachings on Karma, Dharma, and Moksha.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gita Sara?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gita Sara (गीता सार — literally Essence of the Gita) is a curated compilation of the most theologically central and widely loved shlokas selected from across the eighteen chapters of the Srimad Bhagavad Gita. The standard compilation typically includes (1) Dharma-kshetre Kuru-kshetre — the opening verse of the entire Gita (1.1), (2) Yada yada hi dharmasya — the avatara declaration (4.7-8), (3) Karmanye-vadhikaraste — the supreme karma-yoga teaching (2.47), (4) Yoga-sthah kuru karmani — equanimity is yoga (2.48), (5) Nainam chhindanti shastrani — the indestructible Atman (2.23), (6) Vasamsi jirnani yatha vihaya — body as worn-out garment (2.22), (7) Uddhared-atman-atmanam — self-elevation by self (6.5), (8) Man-mana bhava mad-bhakto — bhakti-prescription (9.34/18.65), (9) Sarva-dharman parityajya — the charama-shloka of complete surrender (18.66), and (10) Yatra Yogeshvarah Krishno — the closing victory verse (18.78). (Source: Srimad Bhagavad Gita selections.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Karmanyevadhikaraste in the Gita Sara?
seoFaqs.answer: Karmanyevadhikaraste ma phaleshu kadachana, ma karma-phala-hetur-bhur ma te sango'stv-akarmani (Bhagavad Gita 2.47) is among the most quoted verses in all of world spiritual literature. Word-by-word: karmani eva — only in (your) action, adhikaraste — your authority lies, ma phaleshu kadachana — never in the fruits; ma karma-phala-hetur bhuh — do not become the cause-agent of the action's fruit, ma te sangah astu akarmani — neither let your attachment be in inaction. The verse establishes the foundational doctrine of nishkama-karma-yoga (action without attachment to results): the practitioner has full authority and responsibility for performing right action with full effort, but neither possesses ownership over its outcomes nor may withdraw into renunciation as escape. This single verse summarises the entire karma-yoga of chapters 2-6 and prepares the ground for the bhakti-yoga of chapters 7-12. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 2.47.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Yada Yada Hi Dharmasya verse?
seoFaqs.answer: Yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati Bharata, abhyutthanam-adharmasya tada-atmanam srijamy-aham // Paritranaya sadhunam vinashaya cha dushkritam, dharma-samsthapana-arthaya sambhavami yuge yuge (Bhagavad Gita 4.7-8) is the supreme avatara-tattva declaration of Sri Krishna. Translation: Whenever there is a decline of dharma, O Bharata, and a rise of adharma, then I manifest myself. For the protection of the righteous (sadhus), the destruction of the wicked (dushkritas), and the re-establishment of dharma, I am born age after age (yuge yuge). This is the canonical scriptural foundation for the doctrine of the Dashavatara — the ten and infinite incarnations of Vishnu — including Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama, Rama, Krishna, Buddha and the future Kalki. The verse is recited at every avatara-jayanti and is the philosophical justification of all Vaishnava theology. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 4.7-8.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Sarva Dharman Parityajya — the Charama Shloka?
seoFaqs.answer: Sarva-dharman parityajya mam-ekam sharanam vraja, aham tva sarva-papebhyo mokshayishyami ma shuchah (Bhagavad Gita 18.66) is universally known as the Charama Shloka — the Final Verse — and is considered by all Vaishnava acharyas (Shankara, Ramanuja, Madhva, Vallabha, Chaitanya, Nimbarka) to contain the supreme essence of the entire Gita and indeed all the Vedas. Translation: Abandoning all dharmas, take refuge in Me alone; I shall liberate you from all sins; do not grieve. Krishna here promises that complete unconditional surrender (sharanagati / prapatti) to him alone — beyond all caste-rules, ashrama-duties, ritual-observances and even moral codes — secures total liberation, with the Lord himself bearing the entire karmic burden of his sharanagata bhakta. For the Sri-Vaishnava tradition this is the foundational Mahavakya of prapatti-yoga; for the Gaudiyas it is the supreme verse of suddha-bhakti. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 18.66.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Gita Sara be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gita Sara is most powerful on Gita Jayanti (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi — the day Bhagavan Krishna spoke the Gita at Kurukshetra), Janmashtami (Krishna's birth at Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami), all twenty-four Ekadashis (especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Mokshada Ekadashi which coincides with Gita Jayanti), Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna), Thursday (Guruvar — guru-tattva, since Krishna is Jagad-guru) and Sunday (Ravivar). Many devotees recite the Gita Sara daily in the morning sandhya as the essence-distillation when they cannot complete a full Gita chapter; before any major decision, examination, journey or undertaking; at the death-bed of a loved one (especially the Vasamsi jirnani and Sarva-dharman verses); and after meditation as the closing svadhyaya. The compilation is ideal for children and beginners as the entry point into systematic Bhagavad Gita study. (Sources: Bhagavad Gita parayana tradition; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily recitation of the Gita Sara grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere daily recitation of the Gita Sara: (1) clarity of dharma — knowing right action and right inaction in every life-situation through karma-yoga (2.47), (2) fearlessness about death — through realisation of the indestructible Atman (2.22-23) which the Gita explicitly says is uncuttable, unburnable, unwettable, undryable, (3) equanimity (samatvam yoga uchyate, 2.48) — composure in success and failure, (4) self-elevation through self-effort (uddhared-atman-atmanam, 6.5) without dependence, (5) the saving promise of the avatara (yuge yuge, 4.8) — the Lord arrives whenever dharma declines, (6) the supreme refuge of unconditional surrender (sarva-dharman parityajya, 18.66) which liberates from all sins, and (7) the assurance of victory wherever Krishna and Arjuna stand together (yatra Yogeshvarah, 18.78). (Source: Bhagavad Gita selections.)
